<!-- Auto-generated by `factory update-claude-skill`. Do not hand-edit. -->

<operate_commands label="Operate — pipeline work">

<command name="factory status" short="Pipeline monitor — agents, queues, next actions">
```
Usage: factory status [OPTIONS]

  Show factory status — the process monitor.

  Displays:
    - Agent table: name, model, last run age, idle status
    - Loaded apps (e.g. gsd)
    - Pipeline: non-empty directories with item counts and names
    - Decision gates and pending needs
    - Factory-internal observations by severity
    - Computed next actions (copy-pasteable commands)

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory start" short="Automated dispatch loop (downstream-first)">
```
Usage: factory start [OPTIONS]

  Automated pipeline loop — dispatches agents until idle or gated.

  Reads pipeline state, picks the highest-priority action, dispatches the
  right agent, runs post-execution passes (GC, decision monitor, observation
  extraction, kernel sudo), then repeats.

  Dispatch priority (downstream-first):
    1. tasks/review/        → verifier
    2. tasks/verified/      → librarian (docs)
    3. tasks/failed/        → rebuild + builder
    4. tasks/planning/      → builder (execute plan)
    5. specs/ready/         → builder (plan first if app, else direct)
    6. drafting + research  → advance (spec) or researcher
    7. factory-internal low → auto-promote to inbox
    8. specs/inbox/         → spec

  Stops when:
    - Pipeline is idle (nothing to do)
    - A gate is hit (unresolved decisions, critical observations)
    - An agent crashes or times out
    - A rebuild hits the retry limit (3)
    - --max-steps limit reached

  Examples:
      factory start               # run until idle or gated
      factory start --max-steps 3 # run at most 3 agent dispatches

Options:
  --max-steps INTEGER  Stop after N agent runs (0 = unlimited)
  --help               Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory run" short="Invoke a specific agent (heartbeat if no task)">
```
Usage: factory run [OPTIONS] AGENT [SPEC_NAME]

  Invoke an agent directly.

  Without --task or --message, runs in heartbeat mode (agent checks its inbox
  and memory, responds NO_REPLY if nothing needs attention).

  With SPEC_NAME, resolves the spec from inbox/drafting/ready and sends it as
  a directed message.

  Post-execution passes run automatically:
    - 5 GC passes (specs, tasks, research, decisions, research-done)
    - Decision monitor (detects ambiguity, writes gates)
    - Observation extraction and promotion
    - Kernel sudo (processes config-edit requests)
    - WhatsApp notification

  Examples:
      factory run spec                          # spec heartbeat
      factory run builder my-feature            # direct builder to a spec
      factory run researcher -m "investigate X" # free-text message

Options:
  --task PATH         Task file to assign
  -m, --message TEXT  Free text message for the agent
  --help              Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory advance" short="Advance a drafting spec with gathered context">
```
Usage: factory advance [OPTIONS] SPEC_NAME

  Advance a spec that has unblocking context (research, decisions).

  Gathers research briefs from tasks/research-done/, resolved decisions from
  tasks/decisions/, and the spec itself, then dispatches the spec agent with a
  directed message to finalize. Use this instead of 'factory run spec' when
  context is ready.

      factory advance multi-cli-backend-support

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory decide" short="Respond to hard decision gates">
```
Usage: factory decide [OPTIONS] [SPEC_NAME]

  Show or resolve decision requests in tasks/decisions/.

  Without arguments, lists all pending decisions across all specs. With
  SPEC_NAME, shows decisions for that spec. With --entry and --answer,
  resolves a specific decision.

  Examples:
      factory decide                                     # list all pending
      factory decide my-spec                             # show for one spec
      factory decide my-spec --entry 7.1 --answer "B"   # resolve entry

  Exit codes:
    0  Success
    1  Spec not found or entry not found

Options:
  -a, --answer TEXT  Answer for a specific entry (use with --entry).
  -e, --entry TEXT   Entry ID to resolve.
  --override         Override an auto-resolved decision.
  --help             Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory needs" short="Agent blockers and pending human requests">
```
Usage: factory needs [OPTIONS]

  Show or resolve human-action-needed entries across all agents.

  Reads memory/*/needs.md and displays open items grouped by category, sorted
  oldest-first within each category.

  Note: ``--blockers-only`` is retained for backward compatibility.
  Observation entries are now handled via ``factory triage`` and are excluded
  from this command regardless of flags.

  Exit codes:
    0  Success (including "nothing to show" and successful resolve)
    1  --resolve specified but no open entry found with that ID

Options:
  --resolve ID     Mark the entry with this ID as resolved.
  --all            Show resolved entries alongside open ones (default: open
                   only).
  --blockers-only  Show only blocker categories (permission-change, config-
                   edit, manual-intervention, approval). Excludes observation
                   entries.
  --help           Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory rebuild" short="Re-queue failed work with failure report">
```
Usage: factory rebuild [OPTIONS] TASK_NAME

  Trigger a rebuild for a failed task.

  Copies the archived spec back to specs/ready/, versions the active failure
  report to tasks/failed/{task-name}.v{N}.md, and writes a rebuild brief at
  specs/ready/{task-name}.rebuild.md containing Verifier's remediation
  guidance.

  Builder will pick up both files on its next run, read the rebuild brief, and
  modify the existing artifacts rather than building from scratch.

  Exit codes:
    0  Rebuild triggered successfully.
    1  Missing specs/archive/{task-name}.md or tasks/failed/{task-name}.md.
    2  Spec already in specs/ready/ — rebuild or first build in progress.

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory resolve" short="Mark a failed task as resolved (no rebuild)">
```
Usage: factory resolve [OPTIONS] TASK_NAME

  Mark a failed task as resolved and move its report(s) to tasks/resolved/.

  Moves tasks/failed/{task-name}.md (and any versioned .v{N}.md reports) to
  tasks/resolved/, appending a ## Resolution section with the provided reason.
  Commits the state transition to git.

  Exit codes:
    0  Task resolved successfully.
    1  No failure report found in tasks/failed/ for {task-name}.

Options:
  --reason TEXT  How the failure was resolved.  [required]
  --help         Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory reflect" short="Agent self-examination pass">
```
Usage: factory reflect [OPTIONS] AGENT

  Invoke agents with a reflection prompt.

  Agents examine their own configuration, memory, and pipeline state, then
  write observations to memory/{agent}/needs.md.

  If AGENT is omitted, all agents are invoked sequentially in pipeline order:
  researcher, spec, builder, verifier, librarian, operator, reviewer.

  Exit codes:
    0   All invoked agents completed (some may have written NO_REPLY)
    1   Invalid agent name specified
    2   All invoked agents failed (none completed successfully)

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

</operate_commands>

<inspect_commands label="Inspect — read-only views">

<command name="factory apps" short="Registered workflow apps and pipelines">
```
Usage: factory apps [OPTIONS]

  Display registered workflow apps from apps/.

  Apps are YAML-declared workflows over the factory's agents and bus. Each app
  can introduce pipeline stages, dispatch rules, checkpoint types, and
  execution strategies.

  With --app, shows the full definition: stages, dispatch table,
  checkpoints, strategies, and configuration options.

  Examples:
      factory apps              # list all registered apps
      factory apps --app gsd    # full GSD app detail

Options:
  -a, --app TEXT  Show detail for a specific app
  --help          Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory perms" short="Unix-style permissions (groups, modes, escalation)">
```
Usage: factory perms [OPTIONS]

  Display the Unix-style permissions model.

  Shows groups (pipeline, ops, intel, wheel), resources with owner:group:mode,
  and the escalation policy for kernel sudo.

  With --agent, shows effective read/write access and escalation
  capabilities for that specific agent.

  Examples:
      factory perms                # full permissions table
      factory perms --agent builder  # builder's effective access

Options:
  -a, --agent TEXT  Show effective access for a specific agent
  --help            Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory workspace" short="Directory tree with item counts">
```
Usage: factory workspace [OPTIONS]

  Show the workspace directory tree with item counts per stage.

  Displays specs, tasks, skills, memory, and learnings directories with non-
  zero counts highlighted.

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory logs" short="Recent run logs (per-agent or all)">
```
Usage: factory logs [OPTIONS] [AGENT]

  Show recent run logs from runs/ directory.

  Each entry shows the run ID, trigger type, model, tool call count, and first
  100 chars of the outcome.

  Examples:
      factory logs               # last 5 runs across all agents
      factory logs builder       # last 5 builder runs
      factory logs -n 20         # last 20 runs

Options:
  -n, --last INTEGER  Number of recent runs to show
  --help              Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory triage" short="Manage factory-internal observations">
```
Usage: factory triage [OPTIONS] FILENAME_OR_SLUG

  Manage factory-internal observations.

  Usage:
    factory triage --list [--all]
    factory triage FILENAME_OR_SLUG --promote
    factory triage FILENAME_OR_SLUG --dismiss --reason "..."
    factory triage FILENAME_OR_SLUG

  FILENAME_OR_SLUG may be the full filename (e.g. 2026-02-25T0817-low-my-
  obs.md) or just the slug portion (e.g. my-obs).  If the slug matches
  multiple files, the command lists all matches and exits with code 1.

  Exit codes:
    0  Success
    1  Slug matches multiple files, file not found, or --dismiss without --reason

Options:
  --list         List open observations.
  --all          Include promoted and dismissed entries (with --list).
  --promote      Move observation to specs/inbox/ for pipeline processing.
  --dismiss      Mark observation as dismissed (requires --reason).
  --reason TEXT  Reason for dismissal (required with --dismiss).
  --help         Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory docs" short="Update docs after spec completes pipeline">
```
Usage: factory docs [OPTIONS] SPEC_NAME

  Update documentation after a spec completes the pipeline.

  Gathers the archived spec, verified task, research briefs, decisions, and
  learnings, then sends the librarian to update shared knowledge, skills, and
  memory.  Clears the verified task on success.

  Example:
      factory docs hello-world-python

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

</inspect_commands>

<configure_commands label="Configure — setup and settings">

<command name="factory init" short="Create workspace directory structure">
```
Usage: factory init [OPTIONS]

  Create the workspace directory structure.

  Creates all pipeline directories (specs/, tasks/, memory/, etc.), agent-
  specific memory directories, learnings, universe, and the apps/ directory.
  Safe to re-run — uses mkdir -p semantics.

  Example:
      factory init
      factory init --workspace /path/to/factory

Options:
  -w, --workspace PATH  Workspace root directory
  --help                Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory init-project" short="Register cwd as a factory project">
```
Usage: factory init-project [OPTIONS]

  Register the current directory as a factory project.

  Writes a .factory marker file pointing to the factory workspace. After this,
  all factory commands work from within this directory. The CLI resolves
  upward from cwd to find the marker (like .git).

  Example:
      cd ~/Projects/my-app
      factory init-project

Options:
  -w, --workspace PATH  Factory workspace path (auto-detected if omitted)
  --help                Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory backend" short="Switch all agents between kimi and anthropic">
```
Usage: factory backend [OPTIONS] {kimi|default}

  Switch all agents between kimi and anthropic backends.

  Saves original model/provider mappings before switching so that 'factory
  backend default' restores them exactly.

  Examples:
      factory backend kimi      # switch all agents to kimi-cli
      factory backend default   # restore all agents to anthropic (original models)

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory scenario" short="Manage verification scenario holdouts">
```
Usage: factory scenario [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

  Manage scenario holdout files for verification.

  Scenarios are structured test cases that the verifier uses to score spec
  satisfaction.  Each project gets its own directory under scenarios/ with a
  _template.md and individual scenario files.

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.

Commands:
  init-meta  Create the factory meta-scenario file at...
  list       List all scenario directories with file counts and...
  new        Create a scenario template for PROJECT_NAME at...
```

<subcommand name="factory scenario init-meta" short="Create the factory meta-scenario file at scenarios/meta/factory-itself.md.">
```
Usage: factory scenario init-meta [OPTIONS]

  Create the factory meta-scenario file at scenarios/meta/factory-itself.md.

  Idempotent — running when the file already exists prints its path and exits
  without modifying it, preserving any human edits.

  Exit code: 0 in all cases.

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</subcommand>

<subcommand name="factory scenario list" short="List all scenario directories with file counts and satisfaction status.">
```
Usage: factory scenario list [OPTIONS]

  List all scenario directories with file counts and satisfaction status.

  Columns: Name, Scenarios, Satisfaction
  Exit code: 0 always.

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</subcommand>

<subcommand name="factory scenario new" short="Create a scenario template for PROJECT_NAME at scenarios/{project-name}/_template.md.">
```
Usage: factory scenario new [OPTIONS] PROJECT_NAME

  Create a scenario template for PROJECT_NAME at scenarios/{project-
  name}/_template.md.

  Idempotent — if the directory already exists, prints a message and does not
  overwrite any files.  If the directory exists but _template.md is missing,
  writes the template.

  Project name must match [a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]* (lowercase, hyphens, underscores).
  Exit code: 1 if project-name is invalid, 0 otherwise.

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</subcommand>
</command>

<command name="factory update-claude-skill" short="Regenerate CLI reference skill from live commands">
```
Usage: factory update-claude-skill [OPTIONS]

  Generate skills/shared/factory-cli/ from the current CLI.

  Writes two files:   SKILL.md              Compact overview with required XML
  tags   references/commands.md  Full per-command reference grouped by
  category

  Introspects every registered command, extracts arguments, options, and help
  text. Run this after any CLI changes to keep the skill in sync.

  Output:
      skills/shared/factory-cli/SKILL.md
      skills/shared/factory-cli/references/commands.md

Options:
  --help  Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

</configure_commands>

<gc_commands label="GC — garbage collection (also automatic after every agent run)">

<command name="factory cleanup-specs" short="Remove stale specs (upstream copies)">
```
Usage: factory cleanup-specs [OPTIONS]

  Remove stale spec files that have advanced to a downstream pipeline stage.

  Enforces the single-stage invariant: a spec file exists in at most one of
  inbox / drafting / ready / archive.  When a file appears in a downstream
  stage, copies in upstream stages are removed (archive is never touched).

  Also runs automatically after every agent execution.

Options:
  --dry-run  Show what would be cleaned without deleting.
  --help     Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory cleanup-tasks" short="Remove stale review tasks (verified/failed)">
```
Usage: factory cleanup-tasks [OPTIONS]

  Remove stale task files from tasks/review/ that have been verified or
  failed.

  A tasks/review/ file is stale when the same filename exists in
  tasks/verified/ or tasks/failed/ (active, non-versioned slot only).

  Also runs automatically after every agent execution.

Options:
  --dry-run  Show what would be cleaned without deleting.
  --help     Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory cleanup-research" short="Remove stale research requests (completed)">
```
Usage: factory cleanup-research [OPTIONS]

  Remove stale research requests that have completed deliverables.

  A tasks/research/ file is stale when the same filename exists in
  tasks/research-done/ (the completed brief).

  Also runs automatically after every agent execution.

Options:
  --dry-run  Show what would be cleaned without deleting.
  --help     Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

<command name="factory cleanup-factory-internal" short="Remove promoted/dismissed observations">
```
Usage: factory cleanup-factory-internal [OPTIONS]

  Remove factory-internal observation files when lifecycle criteria are met.

  Removes promoted files whose corresponding inbox spec has been archived, and
  dismissed files older than 30 days.

  Also runs automatically after every agent execution.

Options:
  --dry-run  Show what would be cleaned without deleting.
  --help     Show this message and exit.
```
</command>

</gc_commands>
